_note: SAMPLE DATA — educational planning content, not VA guidance. Family program rules with a 'verified' date were checked against official sources on 2026-07-08/09; program rules change, so always confirm at the official source before making any decision. VetPath never confirms eligibility.

entries.name: SECO — Spouse Education and Career Opportunities
entries.what: Free DoD career coaching, education planning, scholarships, and resume help for military spouses.
entries.scopeNote: SECO serves military spouses — strongest while the service member is still serving, so use it before separation if you can.

entries.name: MSEP — Military Spouse Employment Partnership
entries.what: A DoD partnership of hundreds of employers that commit to recruiting, hiring, and promoting military spouses.
entries.scopeNote: MSEP serves military spouses — strongest while still serving; check each employer's current spouse commitments directly.

entries.name: Federal hiring paths for military spouses (USAJOBS)
entries.what: Federal jobs offer a noncompetitive hiring path for eligible military spouses. Who qualifies is specific — check the current rules on USAJOBS before counting on it.

entries.name: Occupational license portability (interstate compacts)
entries.what: Nursing, teaching, EMS, counseling, and other licensed fields have interstate compacts that can speed up working in a new state. Plain pointer only: look up your profession's licensing rules and confirm with the new state's licensing board.

entries.name: Transfer your Post-9/11 GI Bill to a spouse or child
entries.what: You can transfer unused GI Bill months to a spouse or child — but the election must be requested and approved through DoD WHILE STILL SERVING (generally 6+ years in, with a 4-year additional service commitment). It cannot be started after separation.

entries.name: DEA / Chapter 35 — Dependents' Education Assistance
entries.what: Education benefit for the child or spouse of a veteran or service member who died, is captured or missing, or is permanently and totally disabled from a service-connected condition — up to 36 months of benefits for programs started on or after August 1, 2018.

entries.name: State tuition programs for dependents
entries.what: Many states run their own tuition waivers and scholarships for spouses and children of veterans — rules vary widely by state and change often. See the state benefits category on the Benefits page, then verify with your state veterans agency.

entries.name: MilitaryChildCare.com
entries.what: DoD's official portal for requesting military-operated childcare and fee assistance.
entries.scopeNote: Honest scope: military childcare and fee assistance mainly serve currently serving families — most access ends after separation, so plan the hand-off before you leave.

entries.name: Childcare.gov — find care and assistance in your community
entries.what: Federal directory for finding licensed childcare and state financial-assistance programs where you live — the community option that keeps working after service.

entries.name: PCAFC — Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers
entries.what: VA's most substantial caregiver program. It requires the veteran to have a VA disability rating (individual or combined) of 70% or higher, and the veteran and caregiver apply together with VA Form 10-10CG.

entries.name: PGCSS — Program of General Caregiver Support Services
entries.what: Broader access than PCAFC — training, peer support, and resources for family caregivers of veterans. If PCAFC's requirements don't fit your situation, start here.

entries.name: VA Caregiver Support (caregiver.va.gov)
entries.what: VA's caregiver hub — the Caregiver Support Line, local caregiver support teams, and current program details in one place.

entries.name: SBP — Survivor Benefit Plan (election pointer)
entries.what: Lets retiring members elect continued income for a survivor out of retired pay. The election is made at retirement and is hard to change later — educational pointer only; talk it through with a retirement/DFAS counselor before deciding.

entries.name: DIC — Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (awareness)
entries.what: A VA payment for eligible survivors of veterans who died from a service-connected condition. Awareness only — eligibility rules are specific, and no one should assume they do or don't qualify without checking.

entries.name: CHAMPVA health coverage
entries.what: Health coverage for the spouse or child of a veteran rated permanently and totally (P&T) disabled from a service-connected condition. The P&T determination is required — a high percentage alone is not enough — and a spouse who qualifies for TRICARE can't get CHAMPVA.
